model.fintech_pipeline.stg_transactions.name: stg_transactions
model.fintech_pipeline.stg_transactions.description: Staged payment transactions from the raw payments processor. Casts data types, standardizes currency to USD, and filters out test/sandbox transactions.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.description: Globally unique transaction identifier from the payments processor. UUID format.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: Foreign key to stg_customers. Internal customer identifier.

amount_usd.name: amount_usd
amount_usd.description: Transaction amount converted to USD using the exchange rate at transaction time.

merchant_category_code.name: merchant_category_code
merchant_category_code.description: ISO 18245 merchant category code (MCC). Used for spend categorization.

transaction_status.name: transaction_status
transaction_status.description: Final settlement status: completed, declined, refunded, or chargeback.

transacted_at.name: transacted_at
transacted_at.description: Timestamp of when the transaction was authorized, in UTC.

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_transactions.raw_code:
with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'raw_transactions') }}
),

cleaned as (
    select
        transaction_id::varchar                                         as transaction_id,
        customer_id::varchar                                            as customer_id,
        product_id::varchar                                             as product_id,
        -- Normalize all amounts to USD at time of transaction
        case
            when currency_code = 'USD' then amount
            else amount * exchange_rate_to_usd
        end::numeric(18, 4)                                             as amount_usd,
        currency_code::varchar(3)                                       as original_currency,
        amount::numeric(18, 4)                                          as original_amount,
        merchant_id::varchar                                            as merchant_id,
        merchant_name::varchar                                          as merchant_name,
        merchant_category_code::varchar(4)                              as merchant_category_code,
        payment_method::varchar                                         as payment_method,
        transaction_status::varchar                                     as transaction_status,
        failure_reason::varchar                                         as failure_reason,
        is_international::boolean                                       as is_international,
        processor_response_code::varchar                                as processor_response_code,
        transacted_at::timestamp                                        as transacted_at,
        settled_at::timestamp                                           as settled_at,
        _fivetran_synced::timestamp                                     as _loaded_at
    from source
    -- Exclude sandbox/test transactions that leak into production
    where transaction_id not like 'TEST_%'
      and transaction_id not like 'SANDBOX_%'
      and customer_id is not null
      and amount > 0
)

select * from cleaned

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_customers.name: stg_customers
model.fintech_pipeline.stg_customers.description: Staged customer profiles from the CRM. Deduplicated on customer_id (taking the most recent record), PII fields hashed for compliance.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: Surrogate key for the customer. Stable across CRM updates.

customer_segment.name: customer_segment
customer_segment.description: Marketing segment: retail, sme, enterprise, or premium_retail.

kyc_status.name: kyc_status
kyc_status.description: Know Your Customer verification status: pending, verified, rejected, or expired.

acquisition_channel.name: acquisition_channel
acquisition_channel.description: How the customer was acquired: organic, paid_search, referral, partner, or app_store.

first_transaction_date.name: first_transaction_date
first_transaction_date.description: Date of the customer's first completed transaction. NULL for customers who registered but never transacted.

customer_created_at.name: customer_created_at
customer_created_at.description: Timestamp when the customer account was created in the CRM.

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_customers.raw_code:
with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'raw_customers') }}
),

-- Deduplicate: CRM sometimes sends multiple records per customer on schema changes
deduped as (
    select *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by customer_id
            order by updated_at desc
        ) as rn
    from source
),

cleaned as (
    select
        customer_id::varchar                                            as customer_id,
        -- Hash PII fields for GDPR/CCPA compliance
        md5(lower(trim(email)))::varchar                                as email_hash,
        md5(lower(trim(full_name)))::varchar                            as name_hash,
        customer_segment::varchar                                       as customer_segment,
        country_code::varchar(2)                                        as country_code,
        kyc_status::varchar                                             as kyc_status,
        kyc_verified_at::timestamp                                      as kyc_verified_at,
        risk_tier::varchar                                              as risk_tier,
        acquisition_channel::varchar                                    as acquisition_channel,
        referrer_customer_id::varchar                                   as referrer_customer_id,
        first_transaction_date::date                                    as first_transaction_date,
        is_business_account::boolean                                    as is_business_account,
        credit_limit_usd::numeric(18, 2)                                as credit_limit_usd,
        customer_created_at::timestamp                                  as customer_created_at,
        updated_at::timestamp                                           as updated_at,
        _fivetran_synced::timestamp                                     as _loaded_at
    from deduped
    where rn = 1
      and customer_id is not null
)

select * from cleaned

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_products.name: stg_products
model.fintech_pipeline.stg_products.description: Staged product catalog from the core banking system. Products include debit cards, credit lines, savings accounts, and FX wallets.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.description: Unique product identifier from the core banking product registry.

product_name.name: product_name
product_name.description: Human-readable product name (e.g., 'Visa Debit Standard', 'Flex Credit Line 5000').

product_type.name: product_type
product_type.description: Product classification: debit_card, credit_line, savings_account, fx_wallet, or crypto_wallet.

product_tier.name: product_tier
product_tier.description: Pricing tier: free, standard, premium, or enterprise.

monthly_fee_usd.name: monthly_fee_usd
monthly_fee_usd.description: Monthly subscription fee in USD. Zero for free-tier products.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.description: Whether the product is currently available for new customer enrollments.

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_products.raw_code:
with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'raw_products') }}
),

cleaned as (
    select
        product_id::varchar                                             as product_id,
        product_name::varchar                                           as product_name,
        product_type::varchar                                           as product_type,
        product_tier::varchar                                           as product_tier,
        product_category::varchar                                       as product_category,
        interchange_rate::numeric(6, 5)                                 as interchange_rate,
        monthly_fee_usd::numeric(10, 2)                                 as monthly_fee_usd,
        annual_fee_usd::numeric(10, 2)                                  as annual_fee_usd,
        credit_apr::numeric(6, 4)                                       as credit_apr,
        cashback_rate::numeric(6, 5)                                    as cashback_rate,
        is_active::boolean                                              as is_active,
        launched_at::date                                               as launched_at,
        deprecated_at::date                                             as deprecated_at,
        _fivetran_synced::timestamp                                     as _loaded_at
    from source
    where product_id is not null
)

select * from cleaned

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_events.name: stg_events
model.fintech_pipeline.stg_events.description: Staged user behavior events from the mobile and web apps (Segment track calls). Parses event properties from JSONB and filters bot traffic.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description: Unique event identifier generated by Segment. UUID format.

anonymous_id.name: anonymous_id
anonymous_id.description: Pre-authentication identifier. Used to stitch sessions for unauthenticated users.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: Post-authentication customer identifier. NULL for unauthenticated events.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description: Segment track event name (e.g., 'Payment Initiated', 'KYC Step Completed', 'Card Activated').

platform.name: platform
platform.description: Client platform: ios, android, or web.

occurred_at.name: occurred_at
occurred_at.description: Client-side timestamp when the event occurred, in UTC.

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_events.raw_code:
with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'raw_events') }}
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    where received_at > (select max(occurred_at) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

cleaned as (
    select
        event_id::varchar                                               as event_id,
        anonymous_id::varchar                                           as anonymous_id,
        -- customer_id is null before authentication
        nullif(user_id, '')::varchar                                    as customer_id,
        session_id::varchar                                             as session_id,
        event_name::varchar                                             as event_name,
        -- Extract key properties from Segment JSONB payload
        (properties ->> 'screen_name')::varchar                        as screen_name,
        (properties ->> 'product_id')::varchar                         as product_id,
        (properties ->> 'amount')::numeric(18, 4)                      as event_amount,
        (properties ->> 'error_code')::varchar                         as error_code,
        (properties ->> 'experiment_variant')::varchar                 as experiment_variant,
        platform::varchar                                               as platform,
        device_type::varchar                                            as device_type,
        app_version::varchar                                            as app_version,
        ip_address_hash::varchar                                        as ip_address_hash,
        occurred_at::timestamp                                          as occurred_at,
        received_at::timestamp                                          as received_at,
        _fivetran_synced::timestamp                                     as _loaded_at
    from source
    -- Filter known bot user agents and internal Segment test events
    where event_id not like 'segment-test-%'
      and coalesce(context_ip, '') not in (
          select ip_address_hash from {{ ref('int_blocked_ips') }}
          -- Note: int_blocked_ips not yet modeled, using hardcoded fallback
      ) or context_ip is null
)

select * from cleaned

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_sessions.name: stg_sessions
model.fintech_pipeline.stg_sessions.description: Staged web and mobile sessions from Segment page calls and session tracking. Each row is one session with aggregated page view counts and duration.

session_id.name: session_id
session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Composed of anonymous_id + session start timestamp.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: Customer identifier if user authenticated during this session. NULL for anonymous sessions.

session_duration_seconds.name: session_duration_seconds
session_duration_seconds.description: Total session duration in seconds from first to last event.

page_view_count.name: page_view_count
page_view_count.description: Number of distinct pages viewed in this session.

session_started_at.name: session_started_at
session_started_at.description: Timestamp of the first event in this session, in UTC.

model.fintech_pipeline.stg_sessions.raw_code:
with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'raw_sessions') }}
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    where session_started_at > (select max(session_started_at) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

cleaned as (
    select
        session_id::varchar                                             as session_id,
        anonymous_id::varchar                                           as anonymous_id,
        nullif(user_id, '')::varchar                                    as customer_id,
        utm_source::varchar                                             as utm_source,
        utm_medium::varchar                                             as utm_medium,
        utm_campaign::varchar                                           as utm_campaign,
        referrer_domain::varchar                                        as referrer_domain,
        landing_page::varchar                                           as landing_page,
        exit_page::varchar                                              as exit_page,
        platform::varchar                                               as platform,
        device_type::varchar                                            as device_type,
        browser::varchar                                                as browser,
        country_code::varchar(2)                                        as country_code,
        page_view_count::integer                                        as page_view_count,
        event_count::integer                                            as event_count,
        session_duration_seconds::integer                               as session_duration_seconds,
        did_convert::boolean                                            as did_convert,
        conversion_event::varchar                                       as conversion_event,
        session_started_at::timestamp                                   as session_started_at,
        session_ended_at::timestamp                                     as session_ended_at,
        _fivetran_synced::timestamp                                     as _loaded_at
    from source
    where session_id is not null
      and session_duration_seconds >= 0
)

select * from cleaned

model.fintech_pipeline.int_customer_tran.name: int_customer_transactions
model.fintech_pipeline.int_customer_tran.description: Customer-transaction grain intermediate model. Joins staged transactions with customer profiles and computes per-customer running totals and behavioral flags used in LTV and risk models.

customer_lifetime_spend_usd.name: customer_lifetime_spend_usd
customer_lifetime_spend_usd.description: Running total of all completed transaction amounts for this customer up to and including this transaction.

customer_transaction_sequence.name: customer_transaction_sequence
customer_transaction_sequence.description: Sequential transaction number for this customer (1 = first transaction).

days_since_last_transaction.name: days_since_last_transaction
days_since_last_transaction.description: Days elapsed since the customer's previous transaction. NULL for the first transaction.

model.fintech_pipeline.int_customer_tran.raw_code:
with transactions as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_transactions') }}
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    where transacted_at > (select max(transacted_at) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

customers as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),

joined as (
    select
        t.transaction_id,
        t.customer_id,
        t.product_id,
        t.amount_usd,
        t.original_currency,
        t.original_amount,
        t.merchant_id,
        t.merchant_name,
        t.merchant_category_code,
        t.payment_method,
        t.transaction_status,
        t.failure_reason,
        t.is_international,
        t.transacted_at,
        t.settled_at,
        -- Customer attributes at time of transaction
        c.customer_segment,
        c.country_code,
        c.kyc_status,
        c.risk_tier,
        c.acquisition_channel,
        c.is_business_account,
        c.credit_limit_usd
    from transactions t
    inner join customers c
        on t.customer_id = c.customer_id
    where t.transaction_status = 'completed'
),

with_window_functions as (
    select
        *,
        -- Running spend using window function
        sum(amount_usd) over (
            partition by customer_id
            order by transacted_at
            rows between unbounded preceding and current row
        )                                                               as customer_lifetime_spend_usd,
        -- Transaction sequence number per customer
        row_number() over (
            partition by customer_id
            order by transacted_at
        )                                                               as customer_transaction_sequence,
        -- Days since last transaction (NULL for first)
        datediff(
            'day',
            lag(transacted_at) over (
                partition by customer_id
                order by transacted_at
            ),
            transacted_at
        )                                                               as days_since_last_transaction,
        -- 30-day rolling spend for velocity checks
        sum(amount_usd) over (
            partition by customer_id
            order by transacted_at
            range between interval '30 days' preceding and current row
        )                                                               as rolling_30d_spend_usd
    from joined
)

select * from with_window_functions

model.fintech_pipeline.int_product_reven.name: int_product_revenue
model.fintech_pipeline.int_product_reven.description: Product-daily revenue intermediate model. Aggregates transaction revenue by product, computing interchange income, subscription fees, and FX spread revenue per day.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.description: Product identifier. Composite key with transaction_date.

transaction_date.name: transaction_date
transaction_date.description: Calendar date of the aggregated transactions. Composite key with product_id.

gross_transaction_volume_usd.name: gross_transaction_volume_usd
gross_transaction_volume_usd.description: Total transaction volume (sum of amount_usd) for this product on this date.

interchange_revenue_usd.name: interchange_revenue_usd
interchange_revenue_usd.description: Interchange income earned: gross_transaction_volume * product interchange_rate.

transaction_count.name: transaction_count
transaction_count.description: Number of completed transactions for this product on this date.

model.fintech_pipeline.int_product_reven.raw_code:
with transactions as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_transactions') }}
    where transaction_status = 'completed'
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    and transacted_at::date > (select max(transaction_date) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

products as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_products') }}
),

aggregated as (
    select
        t.product_id,
        t.transacted_at::date                                           as transaction_date,
        p.product_name,
        p.product_type,
        p.product_tier,
        -- NOTE: product_category column referenced here comes from stg_products
        -- This column was deprecated in products v2 schema migration on 2024-01-10
        p.product_category,
        p.interchange_rate,
        count(t.transaction_id)                                         as transaction_count,
        count(distinct t.customer_id)                                   as unique_customers,
        sum(t.amount_usd)                                               as gross_transaction_volume_usd,
        -- Interchange revenue: volume * interchange rate (typically 0.2-1.5%)
        sum(t.amount_usd) * p.interchange_rate                         as interchange_revenue_usd,
        sum(case when t.is_international then t.amount_usd * 0.015 else 0 end)
                                                                        as fx_spread_revenue_usd,
        count(case when t.transaction_status = 'declined' then 1 end)   as decline_count,
        count(case when t.transaction_status = 'chargeback' then 1 end) as chargeback_count,
        sum(case when t.transaction_status = 'refunded' then t.amount_usd else 0 end)
                                                                        as refund_volume_usd
    from transactions t
    inner join products p
        on t.product_id = p.product_id
    group by 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
)

select * from aggregated

model.fintech_pipeline.int_user_journey.name: int_user_journey
model.fintech_pipeline.int_user_journey.description: User journey intermediate model that stitches together behavioral events and sessions with customer profiles to build activation and funnel metrics. One row per customer per day.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: Customer identifier. Composite key with activity_date.

activity_date.name: activity_date
activity_date.description: Calendar date. Composite key with customer_id.

session_count.name: session_count
session_count.description: Number of distinct sessions this customer had on this date.

total_session_duration_seconds.name: total_session_duration_seconds
total_session_duration_seconds.description: Total time in app/web across all sessions on this date.

key_events_completed.name: key_events_completed
key_events_completed.description: Array of distinct funnel event names completed on this date (e.g., Payment Initiated, KYC Step Completed).

did_transact.name: did_transact
did_transact.description: Whether the customer completed at least one transaction on this date.

model.fintech_pipeline.int_user_journey.raw_code:
with events as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_events') }}
    where customer_id is not null
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    and occurred_at::date > (select max(activity_date) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

sessions as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_sessions') }}
    where customer_id is not null
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    and session_started_at::date > (select max(activity_date) from {{ this }})
    {% endif %}
),

customers as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),

-- Aggregate events to customer-day grain
event_summary as (
    select
        customer_id,
        occurred_at::date                                               as activity_date,
        count(distinct event_id)                                        as total_event_count,
        count(distinct session_id)                                      as session_count_from_events,
        -- Capture funnel milestone events
        array_agg(distinct event_name)
            filter (where event_name in (
                'KYC Step Completed', 'Card Activated', 'Payment Initiated',
                'Payment Completed', 'Direct Deposit Connected', 'Credit Applied',
                'Referral Sent', 'Savings Goal Created'
            ))                                                          as key_events_completed,
        max(case when event_name = 'Payment Completed' then 1 else 0 end)::boolean
                                                                        as did_transact,
        count(distinct experiment_variant)
            filter (where experiment_variant is not null)               as experiments_active
    from events
    group by 1, 2
),

-- Aggregate sessions to customer-day grain
session_summary as (
    select
        customer_id,
        session_started_at::date                                        as activity_date,
        count(distinct session_id)                                      as session_count,
        sum(session_duration_seconds)                                   as total_session_duration_seconds,
        sum(page_view_count)                                            as total_page_views,
        max(case when did_convert then 1 else 0 end)::boolean           as had_conversion_session
    from sessions
    group by 1, 2
),

combined as (
    select
        coalesce(e.customer_id, s.customer_id)                         as customer_id,
        coalesce(e.activity_date, s.activity_date)                     as activity_date,
        coalesce(s.session_count, 0)                                   as session_count,
        coalesce(s.total_session_duration_seconds, 0)                  as total_session_duration_seconds,
        coalesce(s.total_page_views, 0)                                 as total_page_views,
        coalesce(e.total_event_count, 0)                                as total_event_count,
        coalesce(e.key_events_completed, array[]::varchar[])            as key_events_completed,
        coalesce(e.did_transact, false)                                 as did_transact,
        coalesce(s.had_conversion_session, false)                       as had_conversion_session,
        coalesce(e.experiments_active, 0)                               as experiments_active
    from event_summary e
    full outer join session_summary s
        on e.customer_id = s.customer_id
        and e.activity_date = s.activity_date
),

with_customer_attrs as (
    select
        c.*,
        cust.customer_segment,
        cust.country_code,
        cust.acquisition_channel,
        cust.risk_tier,
        cust.is_business_account
    from combined c
    inner join customers cust on c.customer_id = cust.customer_id
)

select * from with_customer_attrs

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_customer_ltv.name: mart_customer_ltv
model.fintech_pipeline.mart_customer_ltv.description: Customer lifetime value mart. One row per customer with LTV score, predicted 12-month revenue, churn risk tier, and engagement metrics. Primary source for the Revenue Dashboard customer segment drilldown.

lifetime_spend_usd.name: lifetime_spend_usd
lifetime_spend_usd.description: Total amount the customer has spent across all completed transactions.

lifetime_transaction_count.name: lifetime_transaction_count
lifetime_transaction_count.description: Total number of completed transactions by this customer.

avg_transaction_value_usd.name: avg_transaction_value_usd
avg_transaction_value_usd.description: Mean transaction value in USD (lifetime_spend_usd / lifetime_transaction_count).

predicted_annual_revenue_usd.name: predicted_annual_revenue_usd
predicted_annual_revenue_usd.description: Projected revenue for the next 12 months based on transaction velocity and interchange rate.

ltv_tier.name: ltv_tier
ltv_tier.description: LTV quintile classification: platinum, gold, silver, bronze, or dormant.

churn_risk_score.name: churn_risk_score
churn_risk_score.description: 0-100 churn risk score. Higher = more likely to churn in next 90 days.

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_customer_ltv.raw_code:
with customer_transactions as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_customer_transactions') }}
),

customers as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),

-- Aggregate to customer grain
customer_aggregates as (
    select
        customer_id,
        count(transaction_id)                                           as lifetime_transaction_count,
        sum(amount_usd)                                                 as lifetime_spend_usd,
        avg(amount_usd)                                                 as avg_transaction_value_usd,
        min(transacted_at)                                              as first_transaction_at,
        max(transacted_at)                                              as last_transaction_at,
        count(distinct merchant_category_code)                          as distinct_mcc_count,
        count(distinct date_trunc('month', transacted_at))              as active_months_count,
        sum(case when is_international then amount_usd else 0 end)      as international_spend_usd,
        avg(days_since_last_transaction)                                as avg_days_between_transactions
    from customer_transactions
    group by 1
),

-- Compute LTV metrics. NOTE: Division below will fail when customer_lifetime_days = 0
-- This happens for customers who transacted on the same day they registered (newly acquired)
ltv_computed as (
    select
        ca.*,
        c.customer_segment,
        c.acquisition_channel,
        c.country_code,
        c.risk_tier,
        c.is_business_account,
        c.credit_limit_usd,
        c.customer_created_at,
        -- Lifetime days since first transaction
        datediff('day', ca.first_transaction_at, current_timestamp)     as customer_lifetime_days,
        -- Daily transaction velocity (WILL RAISE DIV/ZERO for same-day customers)
        ca.lifetime_spend_usd / datediff('day', ca.first_transaction_at, current_timestamp)
                                                                        as daily_spend_velocity_usd,
        -- Projected annual revenue from interchange (assumes 0.8% blended rate)
        (ca.lifetime_spend_usd / datediff('day', ca.first_transaction_at, current_timestamp))
            * 365 * 0.008                                               as predicted_annual_revenue_usd
    from customer_aggregates ca
    inner join customers c on ca.customer_id = c.customer_id
),

with_tiers as (
    select
        *,
        -- Churn risk: high if avg_days_between_transactions > 60
        case
            when last_transaction_at < current_date - interval '90 days' then 90
            when last_transaction_at < current_date - interval '60 days' then 70
            when avg_days_between_transactions > 45 then 50
            when avg_days_between_transactions > 30 then 30
            else 10
        end                                                             as churn_risk_score,
        -- LTV quintile tier
        case
            when predicted_annual_revenue_usd >= 500 then 'platinum'
            when predicted_annual_revenue_usd >= 200 then 'gold'
            when predicted_annual_revenue_usd >= 75 then 'silver'
            when predicted_annual_revenue_usd >= 20 then 'bronze'
            else 'dormant'
        end                                                             as ltv_tier
    from ltv_computed
)

select * from with_tiers

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_revenue_summ.name: mart_revenue_summary
model.fintech_pipeline.mart_revenue_summ.description: Daily revenue summary mart. One row per date with total GTV, revenue breakdown by source (interchange, subscriptions, FX), and MoM/WoW growth rates. Powers the executive Revenue Dashboard.

report_date.name: report_date
report_date.description: Primary key. Calendar date for this revenue summary row.

gross_transaction_volume_usd.name: gross_transaction_volume_usd
gross_transaction_volume_usd.description: Total payment volume processed across all products on this date.

total_revenue_usd.name: total_revenue_usd
total_revenue_usd.description: Sum of all revenue streams: interchange + FX spread + subscription fees.

interchange_revenue_usd.name: interchange_revenue_usd
interchange_revenue_usd.description: Revenue earned from payment interchange on this date.

wow_gtv_growth_pct.name: wow_gtv_growth_pct
wow_gtv_growth_pct.description: Week-over-week GTV growth percentage vs. same day last week.

transaction_count.name: transaction_count
transaction_count.description: Total number of completed transactions on this date.

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_revenue_summ.raw_code:
with product_revenue as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_product_revenue') }}
),

daily_totals as (
    select
        transaction_date                                                as report_date,
        -- NOTE: This references product_category which was removed from int_product_revenue
        -- after the stg_products schema migration on 2024-01-10. This model has not been
        -- updated to reflect the column removal and will fail at runtime.
        product_category,
        sum(transaction_count)                                          as transaction_count,
        count(distinct product_id)                                      as active_product_count,
        sum(gross_transaction_volume_usd)                               as gross_transaction_volume_usd,
        sum(interchange_revenue_usd)                                    as interchange_revenue_usd,
        sum(fx_spread_revenue_usd)                                      as fx_spread_revenue_usd,
        sum(interchange_revenue_usd) + sum(fx_spread_revenue_usd)      as total_revenue_usd,
        sum(decline_count)                                              as total_declines,
        sum(chargeback_count)                                           as total_chargebacks,
        sum(refund_volume_usd)                                          as total_refund_volume_usd
    from product_revenue
    group by 1, 2
),

with_growth_rates as (
    select
        *,
        -- WoW growth rate
        (gross_transaction_volume_usd - lag(gross_transaction_volume_usd, 7)
            over (partition by product_category order by report_date))
        / nullif(lag(gross_transaction_volume_usd, 7)
            over (partition by product_category order by report_date), 0) * 100
                                                                        as wow_gtv_growth_pct,
        -- MoM growth rate
        (gross_transaction_volume_usd - lag(gross_transaction_volume_usd, 30)
            over (partition by product_category order by report_date))
        / nullif(lag(gross_transaction_volume_usd, 30)
            over (partition by product_category order by report_date), 0) * 100
                                                                        as mom_gtv_growth_pct
    from daily_totals
)

select * from with_growth_rates

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_funnel_analy.name: mart_funnel_analysis
model.fintech_pipeline.mart_funnel_analy.description: Customer activation funnel mart. Tracks cohorts of users through the onboarding funnel (Register -> KYC -> First Transaction -> Recurring). One row per cohort_week per funnel_stage.

cohort_week.name: cohort_week
cohort_week.description: ISO week of the customer's first session. Used for cohort grouping.

funnel_stage.name: funnel_stage
funnel_stage.description: Funnel stage name: registered, kyc_completed, card_activated, first_payment, recurring_user.

customer_count.name: customer_count
customer_count.description: Number of unique customers who reached this funnel stage in this cohort.

conversion_rate_pct.name: conversion_rate_pct
conversion_rate_pct.description: Percentage of cohort who converted from the previous stage to this stage.

median_days_to_stage.name: median_days_to_stage
median_days_to_stage.description: Median days from registration to reaching this funnel stage.

acquisition_channel.name: acquisition_channel
acquisition_channel.description: Acquisition channel segment for this cohort row.

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_funnel_analy.raw_code:
with user_journey as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_user_journey') }}
),

-- Find when each customer first completed each funnel milestone
customer_milestones as (
    select
        customer_id,
        customer_segment,
        acquisition_channel,
        country_code,
        min(activity_date)                                              as first_active_date,
        date_trunc('week', min(activity_date))::date                   as cohort_week,
        min(case when array_contains('KYC Step Completed'::varchar, key_events_completed)
            then activity_date end)                                     as kyc_completed_date,
        min(case when array_contains('Card Activated'::varchar, key_events_completed)
            then activity_date end)                                     as card_activated_date,
        min(case when did_transact then activity_date end)             as first_payment_date,
        -- Recurring = transacted in at least 3 distinct weeks
        case when count(distinct
            case when did_transact then date_trunc('week', activity_date) end) >= 3
        then min(
            case when did_transact then activity_date end
        ) end                                                           as recurring_user_date
    from user_journey
    group by 1, 2, 3, 4
),

-- Pivot to funnel stage grain using UNION ALL
funnel_stages as (
    select customer_id, acquisition_channel, cohort_week,
        'registered' as funnel_stage, first_active_date as stage_date
    from customer_milestones
    where first_active_date is not null

    union all
    select customer_id, acquisition_channel, cohort_week,
        'kyc_completed' as funnel_stage, kyc_completed_date as stage_date
    from customer_milestones
    where kyc_completed_date is not null

    union all
    select customer_id, acquisition_channel, cohort_week,
        'card_activated' as funnel_stage, card_activated_date as stage_date
    from customer_milestones
    where card_activated_date is not null

    union all
    select customer_id, acquisition_channel, cohort_week,
        'first_payment' as funnel_stage, first_payment_date as stage_date
    from customer_milestones
    where first_payment_date is not null

    union all
    select customer_id, acquisition_channel, cohort_week,
        'recurring_user' as funnel_stage, recurring_user_date as stage_date
    from customer_milestones
    where recurring_user_date is not null
),

aggregated as (
    select
        cohort_week,
        acquisition_channel,
        funnel_stage,
        count(distinct customer_id)                                     as customer_count,
        median(datediff('day', cohort_week, stage_date))                as median_days_to_stage
    from funnel_stages
    group by 1, 2, 3
),

-- Add conversion rate vs previous stage
ordered_stages as (
    select
        cohort_week,
        acquisition_channel,
        funnel_stage,
        customer_count,
        median_days_to_stage,
        first_value(customer_count) over (
            partition by cohort_week, acquisition_channel
            order by median_days_to_stage
        )                                                               as cohort_total,
        customer_count / nullif(lag(customer_count) over (
            partition by cohort_week, acquisition_channel
            order by median_days_to_stage
        ), 0) * 100                                                     as conversion_rate_pct
    from aggregated
)

select * from ordered_stages

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_daily_metric.name: mart_daily_metrics
model.fintech_pipeline.mart_daily_metric.description: Daily business metrics rollup for the Growth Dashboard. One row per date with DAU, transaction metrics, revenue KPIs, and activation counts. Source of truth for the executive daily metrics email.

metric_date.name: metric_date
metric_date.description: Primary key. Calendar date for this metrics row.

daily_active_customers.name: daily_active_customers
daily_active_customers.description: Distinct customers who completed at least one transaction on this date.

daily_transaction_count.name: daily_transaction_count
daily_transaction_count.description: Total completed transactions on this date.

daily_gtv_usd.name: daily_gtv_usd
daily_gtv_usd.description: Gross transaction volume on this date in USD.

new_transacting_customers.name: new_transacting_customers
new_transacting_customers.description: Customers who completed their first-ever transaction on this date.

daily_revenue_usd.name: daily_revenue_usd
daily_revenue_usd.description: Total revenue (interchange + FX + fees) on this date.

model.fintech_pipeline.mart_daily_metric.raw_code:
with customer_transactions as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_customer_transactions') }}
),

product_revenue as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_product_revenue') }}
),

-- Daily customer activity
daily_customers as (
    select
        transacted_at::date                                             as metric_date,
        count(distinct customer_id)                                     as daily_active_customers,
        count(transaction_id)                                           as daily_transaction_count,
        sum(amount_usd)                                                 as daily_gtv_usd,
        count(distinct case when customer_transaction_sequence = 1
            then customer_id end)                                       as new_transacting_customers,
        count(distinct case when customer_transaction_sequence = 1
            and customer_segment = 'sme'
            then customer_id end)                                       as new_sme_customers,
        avg(amount_usd)                                                 as avg_transaction_value_usd,
        percentile_cont(0.5) within group (order by amount_usd)        as median_transaction_value_usd
    from customer_transactions
    group by 1
),

-- Daily revenue
daily_revenue as (
    select
        transaction_date                                                as metric_date,
        sum(interchange_revenue_usd)                                    as daily_interchange_revenue_usd,
        sum(fx_spread_revenue_usd)                                      as daily_fx_revenue_usd,
        sum(interchange_revenue_usd) + sum(fx_spread_revenue_usd)      as daily_revenue_usd,
        sum(gross_transaction_volume_usd)                               as daily_gtv_from_revenue_model
    from product_revenue
    group by 1
),

combined as (
    select
        dc.metric_date,
        dc.daily_active_customers,
        dc.daily_transaction_count,
        dc.daily_gtv_usd,
        dc.new_transacting_customers,
        dc.new_sme_customers,
        dc.avg_transaction_value_usd,
        dc.median_transaction_value_usd,
        dr.daily_interchange_revenue_usd,
        dr.daily_fx_revenue_usd,
        dr.daily_revenue_usd,
        -- 7-day rolling averages
        avg(dc.daily_active_customers) over (
            order by dc.metric_date
            rows between 6 preceding and current row
        )                                                               as rolling_7d_dau,
        avg(dc.daily_gtv_usd) over (
            order by dc.metric_date
            rows between 6 preceding and current row
        )                                                               as rolling_7d_gtv_usd
    from daily_customers dc
    left join daily_revenue dr on dc.metric_date = dr.metric_date
)

select * from combined

source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_transact.name: raw_transactions
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_transact.source_description: Raw data from the Fivetran payment processor connector (Stripe + Adyen).
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_transact.description: Raw payment transaction records from the payments processing layer. Includes all attempted transactions (authorized, declined, refunded). Loaded via Fivetran from Stripe webhook events every 15 minutes.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.description: Unique transaction identifier from the payment processor.

source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_customer.name: raw_customers
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_customer.source_description: Raw data from the Fivetran payment processor connector (Stripe + Adyen).
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_customer.description: Raw customer profile records from the CRM (Salesforce). Includes all customer fields including PII. Loaded via Fivetran every 4 hours.

email.name: email
email.description: Customer email address. PII — must be hashed before analytics use.

full_name.name: full_name
full_name.description: Customer full name. PII — must be hashed before analytics use.

source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_products.name: raw_products
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_products.source_description: Raw data from the Fivetran payment processor connector (Stripe + Adyen).
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_products.description: Raw product catalog from the core banking system (Mambu). Updated nightly via Fivetran. Includes product configuration, pricing, and feature flags.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.description: Unique product identifier from core banking.

product_category.name: product_category
product_category.description: Product category. NOTE: This column was deprecated in the v2 schema migration on 2024-01-10.

source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_events.name: raw_events
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_events.source_description: Raw data from the Fivetran payment processor connector (Stripe + Adyen).
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_events.description: Raw user behavior events from Segment (mobile and web). Track calls with event properties stored as JSONB. Loaded via Segment dbt integration in near real-time.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description: Authenticated user ID (maps to customer_id). NULL for pre-auth events.

anonymous_id.name: anonymous_id
anonymous_id.description: Segment anonymous ID for pre-authentication tracking.

source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_sessions.name: raw_sessions
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_sessions.source_description: Raw data from the Fivetran payment processor connector (Stripe + Adyen).
source.fintech_pipeline.raw.raw_sessions.description: Raw session data from Segment session tracking. Each record is one user session with pre-aggregated page view and event counts. Loaded via Segment every hour.

exposure.fintech_pipeline.revenue_dashbo.name: revenue_dashboard
exposure.fintech_pipeline.revenue_dashbo.description: Looker Studio revenue dashboard used by the CFO and Finance team daily. Shows daily/weekly/monthly GTV, revenue breakdown by product category, and top customer segments by LTV. Refreshes at 7am UTC.

exposure.fintech_pipeline.growth_dashboa.name: growth_dashboard
exposure.fintech_pipeline.growth_dashboa.description: Amplitude-style growth analytics dashboard used by the Growth and Product teams. Shows acquisition funnel conversion rates by channel and cohort, DAU/WAU/MAU, and activation milestones. Refreshes every 6 hours.
